heroCompanyDescription: Evolution Mining Limited (ASX: EVN) is one of Australia's largest gold producers, operating a diversified portfolio of mines across Australia and Canada. The company's key assets include the Cowal gold mine in New South Wales, the Mt Rawdon and Ernest Henry operations in Queensland, and the Red Lake gold complex in Ontario, Canada, acquired from Newmont in 2020. EVN benefits from meaningful copper by-product credits from Ernest Henry, providing partial natural hedging against cost inflation.

skew.rationale: The probability skew is modestly bullish, driven by the extraordinary gold price environment (US$5,013/oz) which provides an unprecedented margin buffer for Australian gold producers with AUD-denominated cost bases. The key tension is that EVN's current price of A$11.89 sits 26% below its 52-week high despite record gold prices, suggesting either informed selling ahead of operational disappointment or a lagged re-rating opportunity. On balance, the commodity tailwind is too powerful to ignore, tilting the distribution toward upside scenarios.

verdict.text: At A$11.89, Evolution Mining is priced for a world where gold's extraordinary run above US$5,000/oz is either temporary or being eroded by cost inflation — yet gold has continued to strengthen, now at US$5,027/oz and up 3.0% today. The stock's 23% discount to its 52-week high of A$17.67 represents either a compelling opportunity or a rational information discount, and the April 2026 quarterly production report will be the decisive arbiter. The DRP outcome and BMO conference presentation provide no hard operational data to resolve this uncertainty. The modestly bullish skew (33% bull / 39% base / 21% bear) reflects the extraordinary commodity environment tempered by the absence of evidence that EVN is actually capturing the windfall in its cost and production metrics.

featuredRationale: The four hypotheses are weighted 30% bull / 40% base / 22% bear / 8% tail, reflecting a modestly bullish skew driven by the gold price environment but tempered by the significant information gaps in the evidence base. The base case carries the highest weight given the absence of financial statements or operational updates that would allow a more definitive view.

identity.overview: Evolution Mining Limited (ASX: EVN) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: Gold at US$5,027/oz represents a structural breakout that is generating extraordinary theoretical margins for Australian producers with largely AUD-denominated cost bases. EVN's leverage to spot prices should drive a material earnings re-rating, with free cash flow generation accelerating sharply and supporting dividend growth and debt reduction. The key question is whether operational execution is capturing this windfall — a question the upcoming April quarterly report will begin to answer.
supporting: Gold spot at US$5,013/oz (+2.7% on the day) represents an extraordinary price environment for Australian gold producers with AUD-denominated cost bases
Silver at US$79.97/oz (+8.9%) suggests broad precious metals strength, not idiosyncratic gold demand, supporting durability of the move
EVN's active investor relations (BMO conference presentation Feb 2026) suggests management is engaging capital markets at a time of maximum price leverage
Dividend Reinvestment Plan activity (Mar 2026) indicates ongoing capital returns, consistent with a company generating strong free cash flow
contradicting: EVN's current price of A$11.89 is 26% below its 52-week high of A$17.67, suggesting the market is not fully pricing a sustained supercycle
No financial statements are available to confirm actual AISC, production volumes, or margin realisation at current gold prices
Gold at US$5,013/oz is historically unprecedented and may reflect short-term macro dislocation rather than a durable new price floor

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: EVN continues to operate its portfolio of Australian and Canadian gold assets at elevated and still-rising gold prices, generating healthy margins while managing ongoing capital expenditure and cost inflation pressures. The stock trades at a meaningful discount to its 52-week high of A$17.67, reflecting investor uncertainty about the sustainability of gold prices and the company's ability to translate spot price strength into reported earnings. The base case assumes partial but not full capture of the gold price windfall, with AISC in the A$2,200–2,500/oz range.
supporting: Dividend activity (DRP outcome and distribution update, Mar 2026) confirms EVN is generating sufficient cash flow to sustain shareholder returns
BMO conference presentation (Feb 2026) suggests management is actively communicating a coherent strategy to institutional investors
Gold at US$5,013/oz provides a substantial buffer above typical Australian gold producer AISC levels, supporting margin resilience even with cost inflation
52-week low of A$6.44 versus current A$11.89 shows the stock has already recovered significantly from its trough, suggesting the worst-case scenario has been priced out
contradicting: Current price of A$11.89 is materially below the 52-week high of A$17.67, implying the market sees meaningful execution or macro risk that prevents a full re-rating
Absence of recent earnings releases or operational updates in the evidence base limits confidence in current cost and production trajectory
Cost inflation in the Australian mining sector remains a structural headwind that could compress margins even at elevated gold prices

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: Despite record gold prices above US$5,000/oz, EVN could fail to translate spot price strength into earnings growth due to accelerating AISC inflation, capital expenditure overruns at development projects, or production disappointments at key mines. The stock's discount to its 52-week high may reflect informed institutional concern about operational headwinds that are not yet visible in public data. A production miss or cost blowout in the April quarterly report would validate this scenario.
supporting: EVN's current price of A$11.89 sits 26% below its 52-week high of A$17.67 despite gold trading at all-time highs, which is anomalous and warrants explanation
No recent earnings releases or operational guidance updates are visible in the evidence base, creating an information vacuum that the market may be pricing negatively
Australian gold sector faces structural labour, energy, and consumables cost inflation that can erode margins even at elevated gold prices
Director interest changes (A Hall, J Klein) in Feb-Mar 2026 could reflect insider selling, though this is unconfirmed
contradicting: Gold at US$5,013/oz provides an extraordinary margin buffer that would need to be offset by very severe cost blowouts to produce a net negative outcome
Ongoing dividend payments and DRP activity suggest the company is not in financial distress
The 52-week low of A$6.44 was likely set during a period of lower gold prices; the current price of A$11.89 already reflects a significant recovery

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: A transformative acquisition, major reserve upgrade, or takeover approach at a premium to current prices could catalyse a step-change re-rating well above the 52-week high of A$17.67. Evolution Mining has historically been an active acquirer in the Australian and Canadian gold space, and the current gold price environment — with gold above US$5,000/oz — creates both the financial capacity and strategic incentive for bold corporate action. This remains a low-probability but high-impact tail scenario.
supporting: Gold at US$5,013/oz dramatically improves the economics of reserve acquisitions and makes EVN an attractive consolidation target for global majors
BMO conference engagement (Feb 2026) places EVN in front of global institutional investors and potential strategic partners
EVN's 52-week range of A$6.44 to A$17.67 demonstrates the stock's capacity for large price moves on sentiment shifts
contradicting: No M&A announcements, strategic review disclosures, or takeover-related filings are present in the recent ASX announcement history
Large acquisitions in a high gold price environment carry integration risk and can destroy value if overpaid
At 8% probability, this is a tail scenario; the absence of any corporate activity signals makes it a low-base-rate event

narrative.theNarrative: [Updated 17-Mar-26 20:05 AEST] The dominant narrative for Evolution Mining (ASX: EVN) in mid-March 2026 is one of extraordinary commodity tailwinds colliding with unresolved operational uncertainty. Gold at US$5,027/oz — up 3.0% today and continuing its remarkable run — represents a price level that would have been considered implausible just two years ago, and for an Australian gold producer with a largely AUD-denominated cost base, the theoretical margin expansion is enormous. Yet the market is telling a more cautious story: at A$11.89, EVN sits 23% below its 52-week high of A$17.67, a divergence that demands explanation. The most recent corporate activity — a Dividend Reinvestment Plan outcome announced on 12 March 2026 and a BMO Global Metals and Mining Conference presentation on 23 February 2026 — provides qualitative colour but no hard financial data on AISC or production volumes. The absence of a recent quarterly production report means the market cannot yet verify whether EVN is actually capturing the gold price windfall or whether cost inflation is eroding the benefit. Silver at US$79.97/oz (up 8.9% today) adds further commodity tailwind context. The critical test arrives with the April 2026 quarterly production report, which will be the first hard data point on whether EVN's operational performance justifies a re-rating toward the 52-week high or confirms the market's cautious discount.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$11.89
priceImplication.content:
At A$11.89, the current price appears to embed:

• Gold price mean reversion: The market is likely not valuing EVN on US$5,027/oz gold on a sustained basis; a blended price assumption closer to US$3,500–4,000/oz seems more consistent with the current equity price, given the 23% discount to the 52-week high

• Cost inflation headwinds: The discount to the 52-week high implies the market expects AISC to have risen materially, compressing the margin benefit of higher gold prices — likely pricing AISC in the A$2,400–2,600/oz range rather than the more optimistic A$2,000–2,200/oz scenario

• Execution uncertainty premium: Without a recent quarterly production report, the market is applying an information discount — the April quarterly will be the first hard data point on whether EVN is capturing the gold price windfall

• Capex drag: Ongoing development capital at key projects is likely being discounted as a drag on near-term free cash flow, even if long-term value creation is intact

• Partial credit for gold strength: The stock is up over 100% from its 52-week low, confirming the market has given EVN significant credit for the gold price environment — but not full credit, suggesting residual scepticism about sustainability

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base remains materially thin on operational specifics. The strongest signal continues to come from the commodity context — gold at US$5,027/oz (up 3.0% today) and silver at US$79.97/oz (up 8.9%) are objectively measured market prices that directly drive EVN's revenue line, and both are moving in EVN's favour. The DRP outcome announcement on 12 March 2026 confirms the company is paying dividends and shareholders are participating in reinvestment, which is a mild positive signal on capital allocation confidence. The BMO conference presentation from 23 February 2026 provides qualitative strategic context but is inherently promotional. Critically absent is any hard operational data: no quarterly production report, no half-year financial results, and no AISC guidance update have been released in the evidence set. The director interest change notices (A Hall on 1 March, J Klein on 17 February) are routine and carry no material signal. The evidence therefore strongly supports the commodity tailwind thesis but cannot confirm or deny whether EVN is operationally capturing it — leaving the base case as the modal hypothesis by default.
narrative.narrativeStability: The current narrative — gold price tailwind partially offset by execution uncertainty — remains moderately stable but is approaching a critical inflection point. Gold has now pushed through US$5,027/oz, further strengthening the commodity case, but the narrative's stability ultimately rests on the April 2026 quarterly production report, which will be the first hard operational data point since coverage initiation. If that report shows AISC below A$2,200/oz with production in line with guidance, the narrative will shift decisively toward the bull case and the 23% discount to the 52-week high will be difficult to sustain. Conversely, a cost blowout or production miss would validate the market's caution and likely push the stock below A$11. The gold price itself is the other key variable — at US$5,027/oz, any sustained move back below US$4,000/oz would fundamentally alter the earnings calculus regardless of operational performance.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality — from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary — ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: Evolution Mining presented at the BMO Global Metals & Mining Conference on 2026-02-23, likely outlining strategic priorities, operational performance, and future outlook to investors. The company also announced a Dividend Reinvestment Plan Outcome and an Update on Dividend/Distribution on 2026-03-12, indicating a focus on shareholder returns.
cards.tension: Corporate presentations are inherently promotional, potentially highlighting strengths while downplaying challenges or risks associated with project development, cost inflation, or commodity price volatility.
cards.source: ASX: "BMO Global Metals & Mining Conference Presentation" (2026-02-23), "Dividend Reinvestment Plan Outcome" (2026-03-12), "Update - Dividend/Distribution - EVN" (2026-03-12)

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: Recent regulatory filings include dividend/distribution updates on 2026-03-12 and director interest notices on 2026-03-01 and 2026-02-17. These confirm ongoing capital management activities and insider shareholding changes.
cards.tension: Full financial statements (e.g., half-year or annual reports) detailing revenue, profit, cash flow, and balance sheet performance are not available in the provided data, limiting a comprehensive financial assessment.
cards.source: ASX: "Dividend Reinvestment Plan Outcome" (2026-03-12), "Update - Dividend/Distribution - EVN" (2026-03-12), "Change of Director's Interest Notice - A Hall" (2026-03-01), "Change of Director's Interest Notice - J Klein" (2026-02-17)

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: Evolution Mining's presentation at the BMO Global Metals & Mining Conference on 2026-02-23 suggests engagement with the broker community, likely influencing their models and estimates.
cards.tension: Specific broker reports, consensus estimates, or price targets are not provided, making it impossible to gauge current market expectations or the collective analyst sentiment towards EVN.
cards.source: ASX: "BMO Global Metals & Mining Conference Presentation" (2026-02-23)

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: Evolution Mining operates in the Basic Materials / Gold sector, implying competition with other ASX-listed and global gold producers.
cards.tension: No specific competitor announcements, financial results, or operational updates are provided, preventing a direct comparative analysis of EVN's performance, cost structure, or market position against its peers.

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: Gold prices (GC=F) have further strengthened, now trading at US$5027.4, showing a significant daily increase of 3.0%. Silver prices (SI=F) remain strong at US$79.97, up 8.9% today. These exceptionally high commodity prices are highly favourable for gold producers like EVN, reinforcing potential revenue and profitability.
cards.tension: While current gold and silver prices are exceptionally strong, commodity markets are inherently volatile. The sustainability of these elevated price levels is uncertain, and any future price corrections could negatively impact EVN's revenue and profitability.

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: No alternative data sources such as satellite imagery, ESG performance metrics, or channel checks on operational sites are available in the provided information.
cards.tension: The absence of alternative data limits independent verification of EVN's operational efficiency, environmental compliance, or community engagement, relying solely on company-reported information.

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: No specific academic research or peer-reviewed studies pertaining to Evolution Mining, the Australian gold sector, or broader gold market dynamics are provided.
cards.tension: Without academic research, it is challenging to assess long-term structural trends, technological disruptions, or independent expert analyses that could provide a deeper understanding of the industry's future trajectory and EVN's strategic positioning.

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: There are no recent news headlines, financial media articles, or social media commentary provided regarding Evolution Mining.
cards.tension: The absence of recent media and social coverage means there is no external, potentially unverified, sentiment or narrative to consider, which could indicate a lack of significant recent events or lower public interest in the company.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: Two "Change of Director's Interest Notice" announcements were made: for A Hall on 2026-03-01 and for J Klein on 2026-02-17. These indicate recent changes in the shareholdings of key directors.
cards.tension: The notices confirm changes in director interests, but without details on whether these were purchases or sales, or the underlying reasons, it is difficult to infer management's confidence in the company's future prospects or potential governance implications.
cards.source: ASX: "Change of Director's Interest Notice - A Hall" (2026-03-01), "Change of Director's Interest Notice - J Klein" (2026-02-17)

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: Evolution Mining announced a "Dividend Reinvestment Plan Outcome" and an "Update - Dividend/Distribution" on 2026-03-12, indicating a commitment to shareholder returns and offering a mechanism for capital retention within the company.
cards.tension: While the DRP provides a capital management tool, the specific participation rate in the plan and its overall impact on the company's capital structure, free float, or institutional ownership levels are not detailed in the provided information.
cards.source: ASX: "Dividend Reinvestment Plan Outcome" (2026-03-12), "Update - Dividend/Distribution - EVN" (2026-03-12)

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity — they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) per ounce in the next quarterly production report -- the single most important metric for determining whether EVN is capturing the gold price windfall or seeing it eroded by cost inflation
rows.currentReading: Not yet available -- next quarterly report expected April 2026

rows.evidence: Gold spot price trajectory over the next 60-90 days -- whether US$5,013/oz represents a durable new price floor or a short-term spike driven by macro dislocation will fundamentally determine EVN's earnings outlook
rows.currentReading: US$5,013/oz (+2.7% on 2026-03-16) -- historically unprecedented, sustainability uncertain

rows.evidence: Direction and quantum of director share transactions (A Hall, J Klein) -- if the Feb-Mar 2026 director interest changes represent net purchases, this is a strong insider confidence signal; if net sales, it is a meaningful red flag at current gold prices
rows.currentReading: Direction of transactions not confirmed in available evidence -- ambiguous

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• Dividend Reinvestment Plan activity: The DRP outcome confirms cash generation but does not discriminate between the bull and base cases -- both scenarios are consistent with ongoing dividend payments at current gold prices
  • BMO conference participation: Presenting at a major mining conference is standard practice for mid-tier producers and does not differentiate between strong and weak operational performance
  • 52-week low of A$6.44: The prior trough reflects a different gold price environment and does not provide useful information about the current risk/reward at A$11.89
  • Silver price strength (US$79.97/oz): While supportive of the precious metals narrative, EVN is primarily a gold producer and silver is not a material revenue driver; silver strength does not discriminate between EVN-specific hypotheses
  • Market capitalisation (not disclosed): Without a market cap figure, relative valuation metrics cannot be calculated and this data point is non-discriminating by default

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

conditions.if: If EVN reports AISC below A$2,200/oz and production in line with or above guidance
conditions.then: Then the stock should re-rate materially toward A$17-19, closing the gap to the 52-week high as the market prices in sustained free cash flow generation at record gold prices

conditions.if: If EVN reports AISC above A$2,600/oz or a production miss greater than 5%
conditions.then: Then the current 26% discount to the 52-week high is justified and the stock risks falling toward A$10-11 as the market prices in structural margin compression

conditions.if: If EVN reaffirms or upgrades full-year production guidance and provides cost guidance consistent with capturing the gold price windfall
conditions.then: Then the bull case (A$19.00) becomes the dominant scenario and institutional investors who have been waiting for confirmation are likely to add exposure aggressively

conditions.if: If EVN downgrades production guidance or flags material cost increases at any key asset
conditions.then: Then the bear case (A$9.00) becomes live and the stock could underperform the gold price significantly through the second half of 2026

cards.name: Gold Price Sustainability Above US$4,500/oz

conditions.if: If gold sustains above US$4,500/oz through Q2 2026 and macro drivers (USD weakness, central bank buying, geopolitical risk) remain intact
conditions.then: Then the entire Australian gold sector re-rates and EVN's current A$11.89 price looks deeply discounted relative to earnings power, supporting a move toward A$17-19

conditions.if: If gold corrects below US$3,500/oz on USD strength, risk-on rotation, or resolution of geopolitical tensions
conditions.then: Then the earnings case for EVN deteriorates rapidly and the stock could revisit the A$9-10 range regardless of operational performance

cards.source: Macro and Commodity Context (Gold spot: US$5,013/oz as of 2026-03-16)

cards.name: Q3 FY2026 Quarterly Production Report

conditions.if: If EVN reports AISC below A$2,200/oz and production at or above quarterly guidance
conditions.then: Then the 23% discount to the 52-week high becomes very difficult to justify; bull case re-rating toward A$17–19 becomes the base case and N1 probability rises above 45%

conditions.if: If EVN reports AISC above A$2,600/oz or a production miss greater than 5% versus guidance
conditions.then: Then the market's cautious discount is validated; bear case scenario becomes more probable, N3 rises above 30%, and the stock likely tests A$10–11 support

cards.source: ASX quarterly production report schedule; EVN FY2026 guidance framework

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

goldAgent.executive_summary: Evolution Mining is a multi-asset Australian-listed gold producer with FY25 group production of 750koz at A$1,653/oz AISC (continuing operations), with Cowal (NSW) contributing 44% of group output and approximately one-third of Q1 FY26 operating mine cash flow. The company recorded a Q1 FY26 record net mine cash flow of A$366m and is accelerating debt reduction, with net debt declining from A$849m (30 June 2025) to A$659m (30 September 2025) and no maturities until FY29. Structural cost advantages from copper by-product credits at Ernest Henry and Northparkes offset persistent high costs at Red Lake (A$2,703/oz AISC). Key information gaps include the absence of the February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement, explicit FY26 production guidance quantum, and detailed asset-level metallurgy and reserve grades.

investment_view.bull_case: Gold price appreciation combined with copper by-product credit uplift drives operating mine cash flow from A$2,755m to A$3,325m per the company's own spot sensitivity. Mungari mill expansion structural cost reduction is sustained at sub-A$2,000/oz AISC, Red Lake grade improvement continues, and accelerating deleveraging restores full balance sheet flexibility well ahead of FY29 maturities. Cowal OPC is executed within A$430m budget, extending the highest-cash-contributing asset to 2042.
investment_view.base_case: EVN delivers FY26 production and cost guidance broadly in line with FY25 trajectory. Group AISC stays in the A$1,650–1,750/oz range on a continuing operations basis. Net debt continues declining through internal cash flow generation. Cowal OPC waste stripping creates a near-term cost and production headwind at the flagship asset. Red Lake remains a high-cost drag but contributes positive cash flow. No R&R degradation on the February 2026 update.
investment_view.bear_case: Gold and copper prices decline materially, compressing margins at all assets and eliminating copper credit advantage. Red Lake fails to sustain grade improvement and reverts to a cash consumer. Cowal OPC capex blows out beyond A$430m or encounters permitting or weather-related delays, impairing the flagship asset's long-term value. Reserve update reveals grade or tonnage degradation. AUD strengthening reduces AUD-denominated revenue per ounce.
what_must_be_true: Cowal OPC is executed within A$430m budget and on schedule to sustain production to 2042
Group AISC remains below A$1,800/oz on a continuing operations basis to maintain strong free cash flow at prevailing gold prices
Red Lake AISC continues declining toward group average through grade improvement and the 'margin over volume' strategy
February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources update confirms no material degradation in group reserve base
Copper by-product credits at Ernest Henry and Northparkes are maintained to sustain structural cost advantage
Net debt continues declining toward zero through internal cash generation without equity dilution
investment_view.monitoring_trigger: Release of February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement confirming group reserve tonnes, grade, and ounces; any Cowal OPC capex revision or schedule delay announcement; Red Lake quarterly AISC above A$2,800/oz for two consecutive quarters

valuation.valuation_notes: No NAV, NPV, IRR, P/NAV or market capitalisation data available in corpus. EV/oz metrics cannot be calculated due to absence of February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement. A partial spot cash flow sensitivity is available: at spot gold and copper prices (basis Q1 FY26 guidance), estimated operating mine cash flow increases from A$2,755m to A$3,325m guidance basis (A$570m upside). Net debt (AUD) was A$659m at 30 September 2025. USD conversion rates not available. Full valuation work requires: current share price, AUD/USD exchange rate, group reserve and resource statement, and asset-level NPV disclosures.

peer_frame.relative_valuation_comment: No peer valuation data available in corpus. Peer group identified by inference based on ASX-listed mid-to-large cap Australian gold producers. EVN's copper by-product credit structure at Ernest Henry and Northparkes creates a cost profile that is not directly comparable to pure-play gold producers. Relative AISC and P/NAV comparisons cannot be made without market data.

goldAgent.management_assessment: Management demonstrates strong operational execution based on available evidence. The Mungari 4.2Mtpa mill expansion was delivered nine months ahead of schedule and 15% below its A$212m budget, representing excellent capital allocation and project management. Q1 FY26 net mine cash flow of A$366m was a record and approximately 20% above the prior record, with every operation delivering positive net mine cash flow. The 'margin over volume' strategy at Red Lake produced a 24% improvement in mined gold grade to 5.15 g/t. Debt is being repaid ahead of schedule, with A$170m in early repayments in Q1 FY26 and a further A$110m post-quarter, reducing gearing from 15% to 11%. The company has confirmed it is on track to deliver FY26 guidance. Specific details on board composition, individual executive mine-building experience, M&A track record, dilution history, and incentive alignment are not available in the corpus and represent information gaps. Overall assessment is favourable based on available evidence, with confidence limited by disclosure gaps.

recommendation.reason: Evolution Mining demonstrates strong operational execution, an accelerating deleveraging trajectory, and a structural cost advantage from copper by-product credits. However, a full investment committee recommendation cannot be made due to critical information gaps: the February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement is absent from the corpus, preventing assessment of reserve base, mine life, and NAV. No NAV, P/NAV, or comparable valuation metrics can be calculated. Metallurgy, recovery rates, royalty burden, and sustaining capex are undisclosed. Red Lake remains a persistent high-cost drag with no quantified improvement pathway. The recommendation is to defer until the February 2026 R&R statement is reviewed, FY26 guidance quantum is confirmed, and a full asset-level NAV model can be constructed.

goldAgent.verdict: Evolution Mining is a multi-asset Australian-listed gold producer with FY25 group production of 750koz at A$1,653/oz AISC (continuing operations), with Cowal (NSW) contributing 44% of group output and approximately one-third of Q1 FY26 operating mine cash flow. The company recorded a Q1 FY26 record net mine cash flow of A$366m and is accelerating debt reduction, with net debt declining from A$849m (30 June 2025) to A$659m (30 September 2025) and no maturities until FY29. Structural cost advantages from copper by-product credits at Ernest Henry and Northparkes offset persistent high costs at Red Lake (A$2,703/oz AISC). Key information gaps include the absence of the February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement, explicit FY26 production guidance quantum, and detailed asset-level metallurgy and reserve grades.

hypothesis.bull: Gold price appreciation combined with copper by-product credit uplift drives operating mine cash flow from A$2,755m to A$3,325m per the company's own spot sensitivity. Mungari mill expansion structural cost reduction is sustained at sub-A$2,000/oz AISC, Red Lake grade improvement continues, and accelerating deleveraging restores full balance sheet flexibility well ahead of FY29 maturities. Cowal OPC is executed within A$430m budget, extending the highest-cash-contributing asset to 2042.
hypothesis.bear: Gold and copper prices decline materially, compressing margins at all assets and eliminating copper credit advantage. Red Lake fails to sustain grade improvement and reverts to a cash consumer. Cowal OPC capex blows out beyond A$430m or encounters permitting or weather-related delays, impairing the flagship asset's long-term value. Reserve update reveals grade or tonnage degradation. AUD strengthening reduces AUD-denominated revenue per ounce.

goldAgent.monitoring_trigger: Release of February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement confirming group reserve tonnes, grade, and ounces; any Cowal OPC capex revision or schedule delay announcement; Red Lake quarterly AISC above A$2,800/oz for two consecutive quarters

hero.embedded_thesis: At A$11.89, the market is embedding a scenario where EVN's operational costs are rising fast enough to partially offset the extraordinary gold price windfall, or where current gold prices at US$5,027/oz are not sustainable and will mean-revert materially. The 23% discount to the 52-week high of A$17.67 is particularly striking given that gold has continued to appreciate — now up another 3.0% today — implying the market is pricing in either production disappointment, cost blowouts, or a view that the gold price surge is speculative and temporary. The absence of recent financial results means the market is effectively flying blind on EVN's actual margin capture, and the current price reflects that uncertainty premium rather than a confident view on fundamentals.
hero.skew_description: The four hypotheses are weighted 33% bull / 39% base / 21% bear / 7% tail, reflecting a modestly bullish skew that has nudged incrementally higher as gold pushes through US$5,027/oz. The base case remains modal given the absence of fresh operational data, but the gold price environment is increasingly tilting the probability distribution toward the bull scenario. The bear case is real but requires a simultaneous gold correction and operational disappointment.

next_decision_point.metric: All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) per ounce, gold production volumes versus guidance, and free cash flow generation at current spot prices
next_decision_point.thresholds: AISC below A$2,200/oz with production in line with or above guidance would be strongly bullish and support a re-rating toward A$17–19. AISC above A$2,600/oz or a production miss of more than 5% would confirm the bear case and likely push the stock below A$11. Gold price holding above US$4,500/oz through the reporting period is a necessary but not sufficient condition for the bull case.

priceDrivers.primary_driver: A reversal in gold prices from an extended peak, compounded by broad ASX200 macro weakness and a sector-wide derating in ASX gold equities, drove EVN's ~23% decline from its 1 March 52-week high of $17.67 to a trough of $13.10 on 15 March, with a partial recovery on 16–17 March directly coinciding with gold reclaiming US$5,027/oz.

primary: Gold price reversal from an extended peak coinciding precisely with EVN's 1 March 52-week high — the most structurally consistent explanation for the selloff initiation and the confirmed catalyst for the 16–17 March recovery
Broad ASX200 macro weakness (-6.7% from 26 February peak to 15 March) amplifying the sector derating across the full window
secondary: Sector-wide ASX gold equity repricing affecting EVN, NST, and WAF simultaneously from 1–8 March, before any NST-specific event
Technical profit-taking and valuation reset after EVN's extraordinary 174% run from April 2025 lows, with reversal occurring precisely at the 52-week high
amplifiers: Northern Star Resources (NST) company-specific collapse of -18.8% on 12 March added a late-stage leg to the selloff, though EVN had already declined ~21% before this event and fell only ~3% on NST's worst day
Elevated volume on the analysis date (13.3 million shares) consistent with active institutional repositioning
background: EVN had run 174% from its April 2025 low of $6.44 to the $17.67 peak, creating a structurally extended valuation relative to historical gold price multiples
Gold at US$5,027/oz on 16–17 March remains historically extraordinary, yet EVN trades 23% below its peak — implying a widening discount between spot gold and equity valuation
The broader ASX200 sustained a ~6.7% drawdown over the same window, providing macro headwinds for all equities
rejected: NST sector contagion as primary driver — EVN's selloff was ~80% complete before NST's worst session on 12 March; EVN fell only ~3% on the day NST crashed 18.8%, which is inconsistent with NST being the dominant causal force

EVN company-specific operational or news catalyst — no company announcements, earnings releases, or broker actions appear in the data; EVN's relative outperformance vs NST (-16.8% vs -29.9%) is inconsistent with a material EVN-specific negative event, though empty news arrays represent a data gap rather than confirmed absence

Dividend/DRP announcements as a price driver — the 12 March ASX filings were flagged non-market sensitive and the selloff began 10 days earlier; these announcements are most likely the source of the anomalous +101.48% reported daily change (an ex-dividend base price calculation artefact) and have no independent causal weight

report.title: EVN: Gold Peak Reversal and Macro Headwinds Drive 23% Drawdown from 52-Week High; Partial Recovery on Gold Reclaiming US$5,000
report.executive_summary: Evolution Mining peaked at $17.67 on 1 March — its 52-week high — and sold off sharply to a trough of $13.10 by 15 March, a decline of approximately 26% peak-to-trough and -15.5% over the 10-day analytical window. The most credible primary driver is a reversal in gold prices from an extended peak, which is directly evidenced by the symmetry between EVN's equity peak and what was almost certainly a gold price high around that date, confirmed by the reverse: gold reclaiming US$5,027/oz on 16–17 March directly triggered EVN's +3.7% partial recovery. Broad ASX200 weakness (-6.7% from its February peak) amplified the move. NST's dramatic -18.8% single-session collapse on 12 March contributed to a late-stage selloff leg but was not the primary cause — EVN had already lost the majority of its decline before that event and displayed notable resilience relative to its peers on NST's worst day. Confidence in this analysis is low-to-moderate given critical data gaps: gold price history for the selloff period, broker action, and NST's specific catalyst are all absent.
report.full_note:
EVN declined approximately 15.5% over the 10 trading days ending 17 March, underperforming the ASX200 by roughly 10.4 percentage points over the same window. The most likely primary cause is a reversal in gold prices from an extended peak that coincided precisely with EVN's 1 March 52-week high of $17.67. The entire ASX gold sector — EVN, NST, and WAF — began selling off in concert from 1–5 March, well before any company-specific event emerged within the sector, which is the clearest structural signature of a commodity price-led derating rather than stock-specific or contagion-driven selling. The partial recovery on 16–17 March, directly coinciding with gold trading above US$5,027/oz at +2.96%, is the single most cleanly confirmed causal link in the dataset and reinforces the gold price thesis on both the downside and upside.

The strongest supporting evidence for gold price leadership lies in the sequencing. EVN, NST, and WAF all peaked and reversed together in the first week of March. Gold's current level at US$5,027/oz is historically extraordinary, yet EVN sits 23% below its peak — a widening discount that implies either the gold price itself was overextended in early March, or that equity-specific cost and operational concerns have caused the market to apply a narrower multiple. The gold price history for February–March 2026 is critically absent from the available data, meaning this thesis rests substantially on inference from equity market peaks and the confirmed 16–17 March data point, and must be caveated accordingly.

Broad ASX200 weakness contributed materially as a secondary driver. The index fell approximately 6.7% from its 26 February peak to 15 March, creating sustained headwinds for all risk assets including gold equities. However, EVN's 10.4 percentage point underperformance relative to the ASX200 over the analytical window clearly requires a sector or stock-specific explanation beyond the macro backdrop alone. That additional explanation is best attributed to the gold equity derating, not to an EVN-specific catalyst, given EVN's relative outperformance versus its gold peers (-16.8% vs NST's -29.9% and WAF's -16.3% from 4–15 March).

NST's catastrophic -18.8% single-session collapse on 12 March warrants careful positioning as an amplifier rather than a primary driver. EVN had already declined from $17.67 to $13.92 — a fall of approximately 21% — before NST's worst session. On that specific day, EVN fell only around 3%, which is the opposite of the correlation profile one would expect if NST contagion were the dominant force. NST's decline accelerated its own idiosyncratic selloff (it fell nearly 30% over the same window), and while some of that would have depressed sentiment across the gold sector broadly, EVN's relative resilience on NST's worst day and its material outperformance across the full window are inconsistent with NST being EVN's primary driver. The precise nature of NST's 12 March catalyst is unknown from available data, which limits the ability to fully characterise the contagion dynamic.

Social channel data — HotCopper, Reddit, and X — returned no usable results. No assessment can be made of retail sentiment's contribution, and no leading or lagging signal from these channels can be identified. Given EVN's market capitalisation and institutional ownership profile, social channels are in any case considered a potential amplifier at best rather than a primary driver.

Two alternative explanations were examined and rejected. First, an EVN company-specific operational or earnings catalyst: the data shows no relevant announcements, and EVN's relative outperformance versus peers argues against a company-specific negative event. The empty news and broker arrays are a data retrieval gap rather than a confirmed clean bill of health, and broker action during this window remains a critical unknown. Second, the 12 March ASX filings for the DRP outcome and dividend update: both were flagged non-market sensitive, the selloff began 10 days prior, and these announcements carry no independent causal weight. They are most likely the source of the anomalous +101.48% reported one-day price change, which is almost certainly a data artefact from an ex-dividend base price calculation error and should be disregarded entirely.

The balance of evidence points to gold price reversal as the primary cause of the selloff, with ASX200 macro weakness as a concurrent amplifier and NST's sector shock as a late-stage contributing factor. The 16–17 March recovery is directly explained by gold reclaiming US$5,027/oz. Confidence is rated low given three critical missing inputs: gold price history for the selloff period, broker ratings and target price revisions during March, and the specific catalyst behind NST's 12 March collapse. Any of these could alter the driver ranking materially — in particular, a major broker downgrade of EVN in early March would immediately displace the gold price reversal thesis as primary driver.

evidence_quality.primary_evidence: ASX price history for EVN, NST, and WAF across the analytical window is confirmed and internally consistent. ASX200 index data confirmed. Gold price at US$5,027 (+2.96%) on 16–17 March confirmed. Two non-market-sensitive ASX filings on 12 March confirmed.
evidence_quality.secondary_evidence: Peer price comparison (NST -29.9%, WAF -16.3% over the same window) provides strong sector context. EVN volume of 13.3 million shares on analysis date is elevated and consistent with active repositioning, though no volume baseline is available for comparison.
evidence_quality.key_gap: Three critical absences materially constrain confidence: (1) gold spot price history for February–March 2026 — without this, the primary driver thesis cannot be directly confirmed; (2) broker notes, rating changes, and target price revisions for EVN during 1–17 March — any downgrade from a major house would likely displace the current primary driver ranking; (3) the specific catalyst for NST's -18.8% crash on 12 March — essential to assess whether EVN faced genuine contagion risk or merely sympathy selling.

what_would_change_the_view: Confirmation of a major broker downgrade or target price cut for EVN between 1–17 March would likely become the primary driver, displacing the gold price reversal thesis and explaining the stock-specific component of the selloff

Gold spot price data showing gold was flat or rising through 1–15 March would invalidate the commodity reversal thesis entirely and require identification of an alternative primary cause — most likely EVN-specific news or a sector repricing driven by cost inflation or operational concerns

Discovery of an EVN company-specific announcement — production guidance revision, cost increase, or M&A — during the news blackout window would immediately become the primary driver given the magnitude of the underperformance

Confirmation that NST's 12 March collapse was driven by a sector-wide issue rather than a company-specific event (e.g., a regulatory change, royalty increase, or macro shock affecting all ASX gold producers equally) would elevate NST's role from amplifier to co-primary driver and materially strengthen the sector contagion narrative

broker_activity.consensus_change: No broker data available for the analytical window. Broker notes, upgrades, and downgrades all returned empty arrays. This is a critical evidence gap — broker action during a 15%+ drawdown from a 52-week high is a high-priority unknown and cannot be treated as absence of activity.

goldAnalysis.executive_summary: Evolution Mining is a multi-asset Australian and Canadian gold producer that delivered 750koz in FY25 at a group AISC of A$1,653/oz, with structural cost advantage from copper by-product credits at Ernest Henry and Northparkes making approximately 20% of group gold production effectively cost-free on an AISC basis. Net debt reduced to A$659m (gearing 11%) as at 30 September 2025, with A$1.3bn total liquidity, no debt maturities until FY29, and Q1 FY26 group net mine cash flow of A$366m (a record). The Mungari 4.2 mill expansion was completed nine months ahead of schedule and 15% under budget at A$212m, whilst Cowal's Board-approved OPC (A$430m over 7 years) extends open pit life to 2042. Key information gaps include the February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement, project-level NPV/IRR disclosures, and royalty/fiscal burden details.

investment_view.bull_case: Spot gold and copper prices sustain or exceed Q1 FY26 levels, converting the modelled A$570m operating mine cash flow uplift into rapid deleveraging and capital returns; Cowal OPC executes on schedule and budget; Red Lake continues its record net mine cash flow trajectory under the margin-over-volume strategy; and the February 2026 R&R statement confirms stable or growing reserve base, re-rating EVN toward sector-leading P/NAV multiples.
investment_view.base_case: EVN delivers FY26 production and cost guidance in line with company confirmation; group AISC remains in the A$1,700–1,800/oz range for continuing operations; Cowal OPC progresses as planned; net debt continues declining toward A$400–500m by end FY27; and Red Lake stabilises as a moderate cash contributor. Copper by-product credits continue to subsidise reported AISC, supporting above-average free cash flow margins.
investment_view.bear_case: Gold and/or copper prices correct materially, eroding the copper credit benefit and compressing free cash flow; Cowal OPC experiences capital cost overruns or weather/geotechnical delays; Red Lake fails to sustain grade improvements and reverts to higher-cost, lower-margin production; and AUD appreciation against CAD erodes Canadian asset valuations. FX sensitivity of A$182m per 5% AUD:CAD move is a latent earnings risk.
what_must_be_true: FY26 group production guidance is delivered within communicated range
Cowal OPC capital of A$430m is executed within budget over 7 years
Red Lake's margin-over-volume strategy sustains grade improvements and positive net mine cash flow
Copper by-product revenue from Ernest Henry and Northparkes remains structurally significant to AISC
No material deterioration in net debt trajectory; continued deleveraging toward FY29 maturity obligations
February 2026 R&R statement confirms adequate reserve life across the portfolio
No adverse sovereign, permitting or social licence events in Australia or Canada
investment_view.monitoring_trigger: Any downgrade to FY26 group production or cost guidance, or announcement of cost overrun on Cowal OPC exceeding 10% of A$430m budget, or net debt rising above A$800m.

valuation.valuation_notes: No project-level NPV, IRR, P/NAV, or peer valuation data available in corpus. At current spot commodity prices, company-disclosed sensitivity implies operating mine cash flow of approximately A$3,325m versus A$2,755m base case, representing meaningful upside leverage. Net debt of A$659m (AUD) as at 30 September 2025 is the primary balance sheet input available. USD-equivalent figures cannot be calculated without an AUD:USD exchange rate assumption. FY25 production of 750koz is the primary production metric available for EV/production oz calculation once market cap and net debt in USD are sourced. All formal valuation metrics require external market data and the February 2026 R&R statement.

peer_frame.relative_valuation_comment: No peer valuation data or cited peer set is available in the corpus. EVN's structural copper by-product credit at Ernest Henry and Northparkes differentiates it from pure gold producers, and this should be incorporated in any peer AISC comparison. At 750koz annual production with multi-jurisdiction Australian and Canadian assets, natural peers would include Northern Star Resources (ASX:NST), Newcrest-era OceanaGold, and mid-tier Canadian producers, but no specific multiples or relative positioning are supported by the corpus.

top_failure_modes: Sustained gold or copper price decline materially eroding free cash flow and deleveraging capacity, given net debt of A$659m
Cowal OPC capital cost overrun or schedule delay extending the period of elevated AISC and negative free cash flow at the flagship asset
Red Lake fails to sustain grade improvements under margin-over-volume strategy, reverting to high-cost, low-cash-flow profile on A$1,795.5m of book value
AUD appreciation against CAD reducing Canadian asset valuations and USD-denominated earnings
February 2026 R&R statement reveals material reserve or resource depletion not replaced by exploration, shortening mine lives
technical_red_flags: Cowal Q1 FY26 AISC of A$2,314/oz is 32% above FY25 average and above group guidance range, indicating near-term cost pressure during OPC transition
Red Lake AISC persistently above A$2,700/oz; high-cost operation with significant book value requires sustained grade improvement to justify carrying value
Northparkes and Ernest Henry AISC deeply negative due to copper credits; gold unit economics without copper are not transparent and create commodity concentration risk

goldAnalysis.management_assessment: Based on available corpus evidence, EVN's management demonstrates a strong execution track record. The Mungari 4.2 mill expansion was delivered nine months ahead of schedule and 15% under budget at A$212m, representing material outperformance against plan. The Red Lake margin-over-volume strategic pivot resulted in a 24% improvement in gold grade mined and consecutive records for net mine cash flow, indicating disciplined capital allocation at a historically challenged asset. Accelerated debt repayment of A$280m in the period (A$170m during the quarter plus A$110m subsequent) ahead of the FY29 maturity schedule reflects proactive balance sheet management. The company has confirmed delivery on FY26 guidance. No negative management credibility signals are present in the corpus. Information gaps include board composition, remuneration structure, incentive alignment, dilution history, and M&A track record, which limits a complete management assessment.

recommendation.reason: EVN presents as a well-managed, cash-generative multi-asset gold and copper producer with accelerating deleveraging, demonstrated capital discipline (Mungari under budget and ahead of schedule), structural AISC advantage from copper by-product credits, and a clear long-life growth plan at Cowal through the Board-approved OPC. The Q1 FY26 record net mine cash flow of A$366m at current commodity prices confirms the quality of the underlying asset base. The primary investment risks are the absence of a current R&R statement (February 2026 statement not in corpus), elevated Red Lake costs, Cowal near-term OPC cost pressure, and AUD:CAD FX exposure. These risks are manageable for an existing holder and do not outweigh the cash flow and deleveraging trajectory at current gold and copper prices. Confidence is reduced by information gaps on reserves, royalties, and formal valuation metrics.

evidence.label: FY25 annual gold production
evidence.finding: EVN produced 750koz of gold in FY25; Cowal contributed 330,008oz (44% of group) and Red Lake 127,632oz (17% of group).

evidence.label: Group AISC FY25 and Q1 FY26
evidence.finding: Group AISC for continuing operations was A$1,653/oz in FY25, rising to A$1,724/oz in Q1 FY26. Company confirmed on track to deliver FY26 production and cost guidance.

evidence.label: Copper by-product credit structural advantage
evidence.finding: Ernest Henry FY25 AISC A$(2,376)/oz; Q1 FY26 A$(1,926)/oz. Northparkes FY25 AISC A$(2,514)/oz; Q1 FY26 A$(2,993)/oz. Combined, copper credits make approximately 20% of group gold production effectively cost-free on an AISC basis.

evidence.label: Mungari mill expansion delivery
evidence.finding: Mungari 4.2 mill expansion completed commissioning October 2025, nine months ahead of schedule. Final cost A$212m, 15% below original budget. Processing throughput increased 48% to 919kt in Q1 FY26. AISC reduced from A$2,753/oz (FY25) to A$1,982/oz (Q1 FY26).

evidence.label: Cowal OPC Board approval and capital
evidence.finding: Board-approved OPC capex of A$430m over 7 years extends Cowal open pit life to 2042. Operation is entering OPC waste stripping phase following Stage H completion.

evidence.label: Cowal Q1 FY26 AISC spike
evidence.finding: Cowal Q1 FY26 AISC rose to A$2,314/oz from A$1,752/oz in FY25, driven by planned bi-annual maintenance, weather disruptions, and open pit Stage H completion before waste stripping for Stage I.

evidence.label: Net debt and liquidity position
evidence.finding: Net debt A$659m (gearing 11%) as at 30 September 2025, reduced from A$849m at 30 June 2025. Total liquidity A$1.3bn including A$525m undrawn RCF extended to 2028. No debt repayments due until FY29. A further A$110m repaid subsequent to quarter end.

evidence.label: Q1 FY26 record group net mine cash flow
evidence.finding: Q1 FY26 group net mine cash flow of A$366m, a record, nearly 20% above the prior record. Every operation delivered positive net mine cash flow.

evidence.label: Red Lake performance and strategy
evidence.finding: Red Lake Q1 FY26 net mine cash flow A$39m (second consecutive record). Gold grade mined increased 24% to 5.15 g/t in Q1 FY26. AISC remains high at A$2,703/oz (Q1 FY26) versus A$2,726/oz (FY25). Strategy is margin over volume.

evidence.label: Commodity price sensitivity
evidence.finding: At current spot gold and copper prices (basis Q1 FY26 guidance), estimated operating mine cash flow increases from A$2,755m to A$3,325m (A$570m upside), and mine cash flow before major capital from A$2,515m to A$3,085m.

evidence.label: AUD:CAD FX sensitivity
evidence.finding: A 5% AUD:CAD FX move impacts net assets by A$182.1m. Non-current assets in Canada are A$1,795.5m (22% of group total).

evidence.label: Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources information gap
evidence.finding: EVN reports Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources annually at 30 June, published the following February. The February 2026 R&R statement is not available in the corpus. No JORC resource or reserve data is available for analysis.

evidence.label: Mungari Castle Hill mine life
evidence.finding: Castle Hill open pit provides baseload ore feed for Mungari for at least 8-10 years.

evidence.label: Mt Rawdon cessation
evidence.finding: Mt Rawdon ceased active mining in Q1 FY26. Group AISC reported for continuing operations, ex Mt Rawdon.

goldAnalysis.verdict: Evolution Mining is a multi-asset Australian and Canadian gold producer that delivered 750koz in FY25 at a group AISC of A$1,653/oz, with structural cost advantage from copper by-product credits at Ernest Henry and Northparkes making approximately 20% of group gold production effectively cost-free on an AISC basis. Net debt reduced to A$659m (gearing 11%) as at 30 September 2025, with A$1.3bn total liquidity, no debt maturities until FY29, and Q1 FY26 group net mine cash flow of A$366m (a record). The Mungari 4.2 mill expansion was completed nine months ahead of schedule and 15% under budget at A$212m, whilst Cowal's Board-approved OPC (A$430m over 7 years) extends open pit life to 2042. Key information gaps include the February 2026 Ore Reserves and Mineral Resources statement, project-level NPV/IRR disclosures, and royalty/fiscal burden details.

hypothesis.bull: Spot gold and copper prices sustain or exceed Q1 FY26 levels, converting the modelled A$570m operating mine cash flow uplift into rapid deleveraging and capital returns; Cowal OPC executes on schedule and budget; Red Lake continues its record net mine cash flow trajectory under the margin-over-volume strategy; and the February 2026 R&R statement confirms stable or growing reserve base, re-rating EVN toward sector-leading P/NAV multiples.
hypothesis.bear: Gold and/or copper prices correct materially, eroding the copper credit benefit and compressing free cash flow; Cowal OPC experiences capital cost overruns or weather/geotechnical delays; Red Lake fails to sustain grade improvements and reverts to higher-cost, lower-margin production; and AUD appreciation against CAD erodes Canadian asset valuations. FX sensitivity of A$182m per 5% AUD:CAD move is a latent earnings risk.

goldAnalysis.monitoring_trigger: Any downgrade to FY26 group production or cost guidance, or announcement of cost overrun on Cowal OPC exceeding 10% of A$430m budget, or net debt rising above A$800m.
